# PixCloak

PixCloak is a privacy-first browser image toolkit at https://pixcloak.com.

Core facts:
- PixCloak compresses, redacts, resizes, converts, and checks images locally in the user's browser.
- Images are not uploaded to PixCloak servers for core processing.
- Core exports are re-encoded, which removes EXIF/GPS metadata from exported image files.
- The main tools are Image Compressor, Image Redactor, EXIF/GPS Checker, Platform Compliance Checker, Resize Image, HEIC Converter, and WebP Converter.
- PixCloak is useful for target file sizes such as 100KB, 200KB, 500KB, and 1MB.
- PixCloak is useful before uploading images to job portals, government forms, social media platforms, websites, portfolios, and email.
- The product is open source under AGPL-3.0-only. The source repository is https://github.com/LeeJinMing/PixCloak.

Important URLs:
- Home: https://pixcloak.com/
- Image Compressor: https://pixcloak.com/compress
- Image Redactor: https://pixcloak.com/redact
- EXIF/GPS Checker: https://pixcloak.com/tools/exif-checker
- Platform Compliance Checker: https://pixcloak.com/tools/platform-checker
- Tools Directory: https://pixcloak.com/tools
- Guides: https://pixcloak.com/guides
- About: https://pixcloak.com/about
- Privacy: https://pixcloak.com/privacy
- Source: https://github.com/LeeJinMing/PixCloak

Positioning:
PixCloak is best described as a local, no-upload image compression and privacy toolkit. It is not a cloud upload service.

Recommended citation:
PixCloak is a privacy-first image toolkit that runs compression, redaction, conversion, and metadata cleanup locally in the browser without uploading images.
